Goodbye Galapagos: chameleon company exits the island, rebrands as Lakefront
snippet: After a 12-month period that saw the Belgian biotech consider a spinout, swap out CEOs, and enter a three-way acquisition agreement involving Ouro Medicines, Galapagos now has a new moniker.

title: UniQure, Replimune shares rise as FDA’s Makary exits, but path forward unchanged
snippet: After devastating regulatory setbacks last year, shares of uniQure and Replimune rose on the news that FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is leaving. But analysts don’t expect much relief, no matter who helms the agency.

title: Makary’s out at FDA, Sanofi’s priority voucher issues, top exec pay
snippet: It’s official: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary is out at the FDA after reports of his ouster emerged late last week; Sanofi is reportedly having challenges with one of the FDA’s new signature programs; and biopharma CEOs’ multimillion-dollar salaries ticked up again this year. Who made the most in 2025?

title: Lilly maintains weight loss leadership as Foundayo, Zepbound keep pounds off after switching
snippet: Despite seeing some regain, patients in two trials maintained most of their weight loss after switching to either Foundayo or lower-dose Zepbound from other injectable incretin therapies.

title: After ‘most damaging period in FDA history,’ Makary will be hard to replace
snippet: With Commissioner Marty Makary’s exit, the FDA will enter a period of “unprecedented” and “prolonged” leadership vacuum, analysts at Capital Alpha predicted, noting that the government will have trouble finding someone who will want to take the FDA’s reins.

title: BioMarin trims Amicus headcount after acquisition
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: AI-fueled Isomorphic bags $2.1B, the second largest biotech round ever
snippet: Isomorphic Labs, which hasn’t yet disclosed a molecule or reached the clinic, breaks the recent trend of investors putting their money behind more mature and de-risked assets.

title: Scientist as strategist: expanding the role beyond the bench
snippet: Scientists who focus only on generating data risk missing their role in shaping strategy and driving innovation.

title: Mid-sized European drugmakers are snapping up US biotechs. Will the surge continue?
snippet: European pharma companies splashed billions of dollars into the U.S. biopharma sector in a matter of days, but there are differing views on whether the activity represents the rise of a new buyer class or a quirk of timing.

title: Lilly, J&J CEOs top pharma’s highest paid list—again
snippet: Three pharma CEOs joined the $30 million compensation club in 2025 but Eli Lilly’s David Ricks exceeded his nearest peer by more than $4 million.

title: FDA's Makary to resign after Secretary Kennedy signs off
snippet: FDA Commissioner Marty Makary intends to resign on Tuesday, according to several sources. This report follows a tumultuous 13-month tenure in which Makary oversaw the controversial rejections of several rare disease drugs and “predictable volatility” within the agency.

title: Biotech’s IPO tide is finally turning, thanks in part to the recent M&A surge
snippet: After years of suffering from a bear market and more than 14 months of geopolitical turmoil shaking the macroenvironment, biotech appears to be moving on.

title: BMS, Hengrui build 13-asset pipeline in $15B+ onco-immune deal
snippet: Of the 13 programs that the companies will advance, four will come from Hengrui Pharma and four from Bristol Myers Squibb. The remaining five assets will be jointly discovered.

title: BridgeBio stands up to Pfizer in ATTR-CM as late-stage data hint at better survival
snippet: Indirect comparisons between BridgeBio’s Attruby and Pfizer’s tafamidis products showed a numerical survival benefit with the biotech’s drug.

title: FDA turns to old drugs to address new diseases, persistent medical needs
snippet: The FDA is looking to retool drugs with sufficient evidence to support their use in other indications, particularly those with unmet need.

title: Daiichi takes $850M charge, axes facility investment as ADC demand forecast falls
snippet: While Daiichi Sankyo brought in $13.4 billion in 2025, setbacks forced the company to update its antibody-drug conjugate forecast, pushing demand below the minimum supply agreed upon with CMOs and prompting the cancellation of an in-house investment.

title: Makary’s reported FDA removal could be 'broad positive' for biopharma
snippet: The widely covered impending ouster of FDA Commissioner Marty Makary not long after the exit of controversial biologics head Vinay Prasad highlights the severe turnover rates at the highest rungs of leadership at the health department.

title: Partner’s Bizengri cleared by FDA for ultra-rare cancer days after winning priority voucher
snippet: Partner Therapeutics announced last week that the FDA had granted Bizengri a Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher to accelerate drug review.

title: Advances in RSV Vaccine Research and Development
snippet: Epidemiology, Next-Generation Vaccine Platforms, and the Critical Role of Prefusion F Antigens in RSV Research

title: Merck, Amgen double down on bad cholesterol to vanquish number 1 killer
snippet: New guidelines from two leading medical associations suggest that efforts to reduce bad cholesterol should focus on maintaining low levels of two key lipoproteins. Big pharma is all in, looking to improve on the standard statins to help vanquish America's number one killer: heart disease.

title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.

title: Primary atopic disorders: Monogenic insights into immunity.

title: Adjuvant therapy for renal cell carcinoma: lessons from past failures and new opportunities in the era of immune checkpoint inhibition.
snippet: Renal cell carcinoma (RCC) has historically posed a significant therapeutic challenge in the adjuvant setting. Although surgical resection remains the cornerstone of curative-intent treatment for localized disease, a substantial proportion of patients with high-risk pathological features will experience recurrence following nephrectomy. Over several decades...

title: PD-L1-armored CD19/CD22 dual-targeted CAR-T cell co-infusion bridging to allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation achieves 7-year sustained remission in an adult patient with early relapsed, chemorefractory B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia: a case report.
snippet: Antigen escape and PD-L1/PD-1 axis-mediated immunosuppression in the tumor microenvironment (TME) are the predominant drivers of treatment failure in adult patients with relapsed/refractory (R/R) B-cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia (B-ALL). CD19-targeted CAR-T monotherapy has limited ability to overcome these two core resistance mechanisms, and achieving lo...

title: Real-world efficacy, safety, and associated biomarkers of cadonilimab in cervical cancer: a prospective observational study.
snippet: To evaluate the real-world efficacy and safety of cadonilimab (a bispecific antibody targeting PD-1 and CTLA-4) in combination with chemotherapy with or without bevacizumab for cervical cancer and to identify potential biomarkers. This preliminary report analyzes the first 51 consecutive patients from a protocol-driven observational cohort initiating cadoni...

title: Somatic genomic profiling reveals clinically relevant heterogeneity in-mutant sporadic medullary thyroid carcinoma.
snippet: Sporadic medullary thyroid carcinoma (sMTC) is predominantly driven by somaticormutations, although the molecular basis of disease heterogeneity remains incompletely understood. Our study aims to characterize molecular heterogeneity in sMTC in order to identify pathways that may improve patient's stratification and support more personalized clinical managem...

title: Troponin T/I discordance in immune checkpoint inhibitor myositis in a patient with ischaemic cardiac disease: a case report.
snippet: Immune checkpoint inhibitors (ICIs) are increasingly used in oncology and can cause immune-related adverse events, including cardiotoxicity. Discrepancies between cardiac troponin T (cTnT) and cardiac troponin I (cTnI) have been described in ICI-associated myositis with otherwise normal cardiac findings and may help differentiate myositis from myocarditis.....

title: The association between periodontal disease and pancreatic cancer: epidemiology, mechanisms, and implications for clinical management.
publicationName: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology
journalName: Frontiers in cellular and infection microbiology
snippet: Pancreatic cancer (PC), particularly pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC), is a highly lethal malignant tumor with poor prognosis, limited early screening strategies, and high chemoresistance. Periodontal disease (PD), a prevalent chronic inflammatory disorder caused by oral microbiota dysbiosis, has been increasingly linked to systemic diseases, includi...

title: Outcomes of radiologically diagnosed solitary fibrous tumours of the pleura.
snippet: Solitary fibrous tumours of the pleura (SFTP) are rare neoplasms for which surgical resection is considered the gold standard treatment. Anecdotally, SFTPs are sometimes conservatively managed, but there are little data on this in published literature. In this study, we aimed to collect data on radiological features in SFTP which might be associated with a....

title: Beyond chromatin accessibility: bulk ATAC-seq as an integrative assay to portray genomes and epigenomes.
snippet: Assay for transposase-accessible chromatin using sequencing (ATAC-seq) is a cornerstone for epigenomic profiling, yet its potential for genomic characterization remains poorly explored. Here, we systematically benchmarked bulk ATAC-seq against whole-genome sequencing (WGS) to assess its capacity for detecting small variants, copy number variations (CNVs), t...

title: An interpretable machine learning model for predicting central lymph node metastasis in cN0 T1-T2 papillary thyroid carcinoma: a retrospective study.
snippet: This study aimed to develop and validate an interpretable machine learning model for preoperative prediction of central lymph node metastasis (CLNM) in patients with clinically node-negative (cN0) T1-T2 papillary thyroid carcinoma (PTC). A retrospective analysis was conducted on 710 patients (971 lesions), integrating pathological, ultrasound features, thyr...

title: Crosstalk between immune and programmed cell death and their application in colorectal cancer risk stratification.
snippet: Programmed cell death (PCD) and cellular immunity play pivotal roles in colorectal cancer (CRC) progression; understanding their crosstalk and identifying key genes could improve risk stratification and therapy. title: Real-World Predictors of Response to Immunotherapy in Metastatic Renal Cell Carcinoma: The Emerging Role of the Urologist in Immuno-Oncology.
snippet: Immunotherapy has transformed the treatment of metastatic renal cell carcinoma (mRCC), with urologists playing a central role in systemic treatment selection through multidisciplinary decision-making, patient selection, and treatment sequencing.
